pattern_description: FORK / CONFOUNDER (A ← C → B): A common cause C influences both A and B, creating a spurious correlation between A and B even though neither causes the other. Tests whether the LLM understands that correlation is not causation, and whether it can identify the confounder as the real driver.
difficulty_notes: Easy. 3 nodes, 2 edges. The classic 'ice cream sales correlate with drownings' example. A model that fails here will incorrectly infer a direct causal link between A and B.
description: Hot weather independently causes both higher ice cream sales and more drownings. Ice cream and drownings are correlated but not causally related.

explanation_points: Hot Weather is a common cause (confounder) of both ice cream sales and drownings
Ice cream sales and drownings are correlated but not causally linked
Banning ice cream would not reduce drownings
The apparent connection between ice cream and drownings is spurious

counterfactual.intervention: Ice cream sales are banned entirely
counterfactual.expected_outcome: Drowning rates would not change because ice cream is not a cause of drowning. Both are independently driven by hot weather.
